text: The SPA console (Service Provider Administrator console) MUST provide a graphical UI for observing traffic, monitoring consumption, tracking issues, and observing budget versus spend.
The SPA console is a separate admin UI from the merchant admin — it is the platform operator's tool for managing all tenants and the entire deployment.
Effort estimates MUST NOT be presented to the owner. No metric other than elapsed real-world time is acceptable if estimates are ever requested.
The primary focus MUST be on quality: correctness, completeness, and absence of defects — not effort.
Azure Communication Services MUST be used as the email provider.
All non-superseded blocked capabilities (including C8 and C9) MUST be included in the release scope.
Launch timing is the owner's decision. Claude MUST disregard any concern for launch deadlines and not use launch as a reasoning factor.
The build stage MUST be executed according to a strict checklist and Repeatable Procedure so that build errors can be tracked and corrected.
The chat widget MUST display on the tenant admin console when the AI Configuration status is Active. This is core product behavior that has been working for 30+ builds.
All issues reported by beta customers MUST be corrected immediately and fixes MUST be pushed to the beta (production) environment with highest priority.
Error messages shown to users MUST be user-friendly — not technical internal strings like '_system: No draft to activate'.

When a tenant admin attempts to activate configuration without a saved draft, the error message MUST read: 'Save your configuration first. Go to Agent Configuration, review your settings, and click Save draft inputs before activating.'
Tier-gated features (e.g., Memory & Privacy pro+ fields) MUST NOT be submitted to the API when the tenant is on a lower tier. The frontend MUST filter out tier-gated fields before save.
When a save operation fails, the actual error from the backend MUST be surfaced to the user — not a generic 'Failed to save settings' message.
The admin console MUST have a favicon.
Team member invitation emails MUST be sent when a team member is invited. The system MUST NOT rely on the admin manually sharing API keys.
A Protected Behaviors registry MUST be maintained to prevent uncontrolled regressions. Behaviors with machine-verifiable assertions MUST be checked before every build.
Administrators MUST have 2FA enabled by default, with explicit opt-out only if the superadmin allows it.
2FA MUST support two options: (1) SMS and (2) authenticator app (e.g., MS Authenticator, Google Authenticator).
Escalation agents (non-administrators) do NOT require 2FA because they cannot alter system configuration.
Administrators MUST have all of the same access as the superadmin, with the exception that the superadmin account is not visible to any other user and cannot be disabled.
All non-administrators MUST only have permission to interact with the inbox.
Auth, 2FA, and RBAC are tightly coupled and MUST be dealt with at the same time as a unified implementation.
The superadmin MUST either register a telephone number for 2FA or 2FA enrollment MUST be enforced before the first activation, with an explicit opt-out choice if they want to bypass it.
Auth flow MUST be: email address to get the magic link, then 2FA when you land on the auth gate. No passwords.
A time series chart (WI #300) MUST be developed showing improvements over time with annotations for specific changes in working practices (e.g., knowledge base introduction, database introduction).
The 1.0 release MUST be a full GA release for general availability — not a beta or soft launch.
The Agent Red website MUST initially be hosted within the remakerdigital.com domain (under an 'Applications' category). A standalone Agent Red website on Azure is deferred to the 1.1 release.
The GitHub wiki MUST use diagrams, charts, tables, mermaid and other graphical representations wherever possible. The wiki will be used by design and UX consultants to understand the system.
Affiliate revenue from third-party integrations is deferred until after launch.
Team member deletion MUST be a hard delete — no soft delete.
All team member actions MUST be stored in an audit log.
The Status checkbox MUST be removed from team management.
Configuration MUST NOT be 'Active' when mandatory inputs are missing. The system MUST prevent activation without all required fields.
The seed script MUST initialize tenants with config_state='draft', NOT 'active'.
The 'Saved configurations' feature MUST be removed from the Configuration page (vestigial).
The 'Preview' panel MUST be removed from the Configuration page.
Deployment, tests, tenant provisioning, and Azure environment setup MUST be governed by Repeatable Procedures (structured SOPs with pinned variables, verification gates, and known failure modes).
When errors occur during a Repeatable Procedure, the error MUST be treated as informative — the procedure itself MUST be corrected, not just the immediate issue.
The system MUST support a minimum of 50 concurrent tenants at launch.
SPA = Service Provider Administrator. Each merchant tenant has a superadmin plus administrators. The SPA console is the owner's cross-tenant management tool.
Complete testing of everything already implemented is the top priority — higher than implementing new features.
After testing is complete, implement new UI/UX features to close the gap with competitors.
The entire system MUST be tested in the production Azure environment before testing is considered complete.
The Azure OpenAI Service resource in East US 2 is exclusively for Agent Red. No other applications share these credentials.
The .env.local file MUST be kept up-to-date for production deployment at all times.
Performance and load tests, Azure provisioning/scaling/multitenancy/monitoring/metering tests MUST be included in the test plan.
Session wrap-up MUST include: knowledge base updates, cleanup of unnecessary artifacts, commit and push to GitHub, GitHub project/wiki updates, and a next-session initialization prompt.
The admin UI API version numbering MUST track the API version so administrators can report the specific version when encountering issues.
Documentation tooltip links MUST resolve directly to the specific anchored content on the target docs page, not to the page as a whole.
Documentation links that are broken or return 404 MUST be treated as a significant concern requiring immediate remediation.
When creating a new tenant, the environment MUST be seeded with default values via a seed process. The seed script serves as both initialization and functional evaluation.
Service recovery documentation MUST retain all information needed to recreate the environment from scratch, including noting any key invalidation that would affect customers.
The system MUST be recoverable from a catastrophic failure (like Azure account termination) with no major impact to customers.
End-to-end automated tests MUST verify the setup is complete and correct AND baseline performance on a freshly initialized system.
The chat widget MUST NOT display on the admin console when AI Configuration is not Active. The widget visibility MUST be tied to the activation status.
Dashboard 'Conversation volume' data MUST NOT show data from prior to the system's initialization date. Data from a prior environment MUST NOT leak into a freshly reconstructed system.
Dashboard metrics (e.g., escalation count) MUST accurately reflect actual data. If no escalated messages exist in the system, the dashboard MUST show 0 escalated.
All tooltip links across the admin UI MUST resolve to page-specific anchors on the documentation site, not to the generic page.
A color palette worksheet MUST be created for the graphic designer showing all UI element types with their current hex colors in both Dark/Light mode, with input fields for new values.
A Remaker Digital Shopify storefront MUST be considered for dual purpose: selling Agent Red subscriptions AND deploying Agent Red as the store's chat system (live demo).
All remaining 1.0 release work items that are not blocked by owner input MUST proceed in parallel while the owner works on blocking items.
Provider Admin Monitoring requirements RB-1, RB-2, RB-7, RB-8 MUST remain Release-Blocking priority.
Provider Admin Monitoring requirements C-1, C-2, C-3, C-4 MUST remain Critical priority.
Provider Admin Monitoring requirements HV-5 (status page) and HV-3 (integration reliability) MUST be upgraded to Critical priority.
Memory issues (uncontrolled memory loss, context drift) MUST be treated as CRITICAL priority and diagnosed immediately when detected.
Uncontrolled regressions (features silently removed, tests silently dropped from procedures) MUST be prevented by a Protected Behaviors strategy.
Code, tests, features, and procedure entries MUST NEVER be removed without explicit owner approval in the current session.
The Docusaurus docs site MUST serve pages under the /docs/ URL prefix. Admin UI tooltip links use /docs/* URLs.
VITE_API_URL MUST be empty when building admin SPAs for Docker deployment, so SPAs use same-origin relative URLs. The build procedure MUST clear this variable before building.
